meaning: n.[总称]<主美>公共交通工具乘客(人数)
examples: Five years ago we discontinued train service between Lamberton and its suburbs because low ridership caused total fares collected to be substantially lower than the cost of operating the service.
Ridership on Amtrak has jumped by 18% over the past two years.
But LYNX attracts only 20,000 daily riders, about 4% the ridership rate of New York City's subway system.
And the Chicago Transit Authority has increased ridership with real-time bus tracking and arrival times.
Some urban rail and bus services have reported increased ridership as gas prices have gone up.
